description: Document this plan as a follow-up remediation to `2026-04-21-premium-abac-authorization-kernel` and map each remediation item back to the original feature/test IDs it corrects.
Change draft-editor read flows so they do not create draft revisions or any other write-side effects for read-only users.
Scope bundle rule updates to the target tenant, bundle, and active draft revision so published or unrelated rules cannot be mutated through draft actions.
Scope bundle rule deletes to the target tenant, bundle, and active draft revision so published or unrelated rules cannot be removed through draft actions.
Add or tighten data-model integrity constraints needed to support draft/publish isolation where the current schema does not fully enforce revision/bundle consistency.
Persist, normalize, and load `selectedClientIds` from bundle rule config into runtime bundle-rule evaluation.
Persist, normalize, and load `selectedBoardIds` from bundle rule config into runtime bundle-rule evaluation.
Update bundle-provider evaluation so `selected_clients` and `selected_boards` templates use rule-level configured IDs during runtime authorization decisions.
Publish the initial revision when seeding starter bundles so seeded bundles are immediately enforceable through the normal published-revision path.
Align migrated time/delegation premium bundle evaluation with the configured Time resource key used by the bundle catalog/editor.
Correct simulator billing-record lookup so it uses the same record family as the migrated billing authorization path under review.
Improve simulator fidelity so supported resource-family simulations include the relevant builtin resource-specific invariants rather than only RBAC plus bundle overlays.

If builtin simulator fidelity cannot be fully implemented for some resource families in this follow-up, explicitly constrain simulator support so unsupported scenarios are not presented as trustworthy.
Normalize API authorization subject shaping so client and portfolio identifiers are read consistently from the API user context used in kernel evaluation.
Remediate migrated API ticket list pagination so authorization narrowing does not produce misleading totals or unreachable authorized records.
Remediate migrated API project list pagination so authorization narrowing does not produce misleading totals or unreachable authorized records.
Remediate migrated API quote list pagination so authorization narrowing does not produce misleading totals or unreachable authorized records.
Preserve fail-closed behavior while fixing API pagination semantics; remediation must not broaden access or leak unauthorized totals.
Add regression coverage proving draft-only rule mutations cannot touch published or out-of-scope rules.
Add regression coverage proving selected-client and selected-board configured rule values are honored by runtime bundle evaluation.
Add regression coverage proving seeded starter bundles create enforceable published revisions.
Add regression coverage proving premium Time bundle rules match and narrow the migrated time/delegation paths as intended.
Add regression coverage proving simulator billing lookup and builtin-rule fidelity are aligned with supported runtime behavior.
Add regression coverage proving migrated API list pagination semantics remain coherent under authorization narrowing for tickets, projects, and quotes.
